pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 33: INSERT INTO students (student_id, first_name, ...) SELECT 9001, 'Maria', 'Garcia', ...
setup_task.sh line 33: INSERT INTO students (...) SELECT 9001, 'Maria', 'Garcia', ...
setup_task.sh line 44: INSERT INTO courses (..., title, ...) SELECT 8001, @syear, 1, 'AP Biology', 'BIO-201', ...
setup_task.sh line 44: INSERT INTO courses (...) SELECT ..., 'AP Biology', 'BIO-201', ... WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM courses WHERE short_name='BIO-201')
setup_task.sh line 33 (student) and line 45 (course): Both INSERT statements specify grade_level='10'
direct_db_setup.sh line 11: ADMIN_USER="admin" and line 127: INSERT INTO login_authentication (..., username, ...) VALUES (1, 1, 'admin', ...)
direct_db_setup.sh line 12: ADMIN_PASS="Admin@123" and line 65-67 where this password is hashed and inserted into login_authentication table

privileged_info_summary: The task enrolls a synthetic student named Maria Garcia (Grade 10, birthdate 2008-05-14, student ID 9001) into a synthetic course 'AP Biology' (course code BIO-201, course ID 8001) within the OpenSIS v9.2 student information system. All student and course data is programmatically created during task setup via SQL INSERT statements. The admin login credentials are username 'admin' with password 'Admin@123', established in the login_authentication table during environment initialization. The task requires an authenticated admin user to navigate the Student Schedules module and add the course section (BIO-201-01, course_period_id 7001) to the student's schedule.
